dependent child means a person who was a dependent child of the applicant for the benefit at any time during the average income calculation period

income, for a person, excludes any child support that is an interest acquired by the person (for example, an interest acquired that is an offset child support payment, or an interest acquired by way of any child support debt recovery by reduced further payments of child support), but includes—
any money received by the person that is child support and—

is not a payment that is ordered by the Family Court under section 109 of the Child Support Act 1991 (orders for provision of child support in form of lump sum) and that MSD determines, under clause 3(b) of Schedule 3 of the Act, is an interest in capital received or acquired by the person; and

may, but need not, be a child support excess payment (which includes, without limitation, a mixed child support payment) that, under clause 8(na) of Schedule 3 of the Act, is not income of the person; and
may, but need not, be a payment that, under clause 9 of Schedule 3 of the Act and regulation 294 (income exemptions), is not income of the person; and
is an information share child support payment or a general provisions child support payment:
the amounts received by the person of any of the following benefits:
jobseeker support:
sole parent support:
supported living payment:
youth payment:
young parent payment:
emergency benefit:
accommodation supplement under section 65 of the Act:
widow’s benefit and domestic purposes benefit (as saved by clause 55 of Schedule 1 of the Act):
the amount of any tax withheld or deducted under section RD 11(3) of the Income Tax Act 2007 for all or any of those benefits
redundancy payment means a payment (before the deduction of income tax) made in relation to the termination of a person’s employment if—
either—
the main reason for the termination is that the person’s position is or will be superfluous to the employer’s needs, and the person is not a seasonal worker; or
the person’s usual seasonal employment is not made available by the employer mainly because the person’s position, or usual position, is or will be superfluous to the employer’s needs; and
the payment is not a specified excluded payment or a retirement payment
relevant survey, for the purposes of the definition in this regulation of average wage, means—
the latest Quarterly Employment Survey published by Statistics New Zealand; or
if the survey referred to in paragraph (a) ceases to be published, any measure certified by the Government Statistician as an equivalent to that survey

retirement payment means a payment (before the deduction of income tax) that is made because of the termination of a person’s employment on the person’s retirement and paid in 1 or more lump sums or on a periodical basis, and that is not a specified excluded payment or a payment from a superannuation fund or other similar payment
specified excluded payment means a payment that is any of the following:
a payment solely because of a seasonal lay-off:
a payment that depends on the completion of—
a fixed-term engagement; or
an engagement to complete work specified in a contract:
a payment instead of notice terminating the employment:
any payment (including holiday pay) if MSD considers that, but for the termination of the employment, it would have been paid as monetary remuneration of the person:
any payment made by a company (within the meaning of the Companies Act 1993) to a director of the company under section 161 of the Companies Act 1993
specified income, in relation to a person, means—
the person’s income in the average income calculation period; and
the amount of any redundancy payment or retirement payment, before the deduction of income tax, not included in the person’s income under paragraph (a) and made to the person—
before the person’s employment ceased; or
in the 52 weeks after the date on which the employment ceased.

history.history-note: Regulation 182 income: amended, on 1 July 2023, by section 54(1) of the Child Support (Pass On) Acts Amendment Act 2023 (2023 No 27).
Regulation 182 income paragraph (aa): inserted, on 1 July 2023, by section 54(2) of the Child Support (Pass On) Acts Amendment Act 2023 (2023 No 27).
Regulation 182 relevant survey paragraph (b): replaced, on 1 September 2022, by section 107(1) of the Data and Statistics Act 2022 (2022 No 39).

prov.heading: Period of benefit stand down: general

para.text: The stand-down period for a benefit to which section 316 of the Act applies (see sections 313, 314, and 316(1)﻿(b) of the Act) is,—
for a person who is in a relationship,—
with average income less than the average wage plus $80 for each dependent child, 1 week; or
in any other case, 2 weeks; and
for a single person,—
with average income less than the average wage minus $80 plus $80 for each dependent child, 1 week; and
in any other case, 2 weeks.

A person who is entitled to a benefit to which section 316(1) of the Act applies (see sections 313 and 314 of the Act) may elect to have the person’s average income calculated by reference to a period of 52 weeks instead of 26 weeks.

prov.heading: Delayed redundancy and retirement payments

para.text: This regulation applies to a person who has applied for a benefit of a kind specified in section 313(1) or 314(1) of the Act if—
the person is entitled to receive a redundancy payment or a retirement payment; and
the person has not received the whole or a part of that payment.

The person is entitled to receive the benefit applied for on a provisional basis and to have the stand-down period calculated as if the person’s average income did not include the amount not yet received.

If the person receives later, but within 52 weeks after the date on which the person’s employment ceased, the whole or a part of the redundancy payment or retirement payment, then this subclause applies to any payment of the benefit that is a payment—
that is received by the person; and
to which the person would not be entitled but for subclause (2).
A payment to which subclause (3) applies is a debt due to the Crown and subject to recovery under section 362(1) (MSD’s duty to recover debts) of the Act by MSD from the person.

prov.heading: Seasonal workers made redundant after benefit commences

para.text: This regulation applies to a person who is a seasonal worker if the person—
applies following a seasonal layoff for a benefit of a kind specified in section 313(1) of the Act; and
becomes entitled to receive a redundancy payment because the person’s usual seasonal employment is made unavailable by the employer after the benefit commences; and
receives the whole or a part of the redundancy payment within 52 weeks after the date on which the person’s seasonal employment ceased.
This subclause applies to any payment of the benefit that is a payment—
that is received by the person; and
to which the person would not have been entitled if the redundancy payment received had been taken into account in calculating the stand-down period under regulation 183.
A payment to which subclause (2) applies is a debt due to the Crown and subject to recovery under section 362(1) (MSD’s duty to recover debts) of the Act by MSD from the person.

subpart.heading: Expiry and regrant of benefits

prov.heading: Certain benefits not subject to benefit expiry and regrant provisions

para.text: The following benefits are not specified benefits for the purposes of sections 331 to 336 of the Act:
a supported living payment on the ground of restricted work capacity or total blindness, under section 34 of the Act:
a supported living payment on the ground of caring for another person, under section 40 of the Act:
an emergency benefit.

prov.heading: Expiry date for jobseeker support

prov.body.subprov.crosshead: General rule: 26 weeks after date benefit commenced or last commenced

para.text: The expiry date for jobseeker support for the purposes of sections 331 to 336 of the Act is (subject to subclauses (2) and (3)) the date that is 26 weeks after the date on which the person’s jobseeker support commenced or, as the case may be, last commenced.

prov.body.subprov.crosshead: Exception: if sole parent support replaced with jobseeker support

para.text: For a beneficiary whose sole parent support is replaced with jobseeker support under section 33 of the Act when the beneficiary’s youngest dependent child turns 14 years old, that expiry date is the earlier of—
the 12-month anniversary of the date on which the beneficiary’s original benefit commenced or, as the case may be, last commenced; and
the date that is 26 weeks after the beneficiary’s jobseeker support commenced.
For the purpose of subclause (2), the original benefit is,—

for a beneficiary who transferred to sole parent support on 15 July 2013 under clause 2(6) to (8) or (9) and (11) of Schedule 32 of the Social Security Act 1964, either a domestic purposes benefit under section 27B of that Act or a widow’s benefit under section 21 of that Act, as the case may be; and
for any other beneficiary, sole parent support.

history.history-note: Regulation 187(1) heading: amended, on 1 July 2025, by section 70(1) of the Social Security Amendment Act 2025 (2025 No 25).
Regulation 187(1): amended, on 1 July 2025, by section 70(2) of the Social Security Amendment Act 2025 (2025 No 25).
Regulation 187(2): replaced, on 1 July 2025, by section 70(3) of the Social Security Amendment Act 2025 (2025 No 25).
Regulation 187(4) heading: revoked, on 1 July 2025, pursuant to section 70(4) of the Social Security Amendment Act 2025 (2025 No 25).
Regulation 187(4): revoked, on 1 July 2025, by section 70(4) of the Social Security Amendment Act 2025 (2025 No 25).
Regulation 187(5) heading: revoked, on 1 July 2025, pursuant to section 70(4) of the Social Security Amendment Act 2025 (2025 No 25).
Regulation 187(5): revoked, on 1 July 2025, by section 70(4) of the Social Security Amendment Act 2025 (2025 No 25).
Regulation 187(6) heading: revoked, on 1 July 2025, pursuant to section 70(4) of the Social Security Amendment Act 2025 (2025 No 25).
Regulation 187(6): revoked, on 1 July 2025, by section 70(4) of the Social Security Amendment Act 2025 (2025 No 25).

prov.heading: Expiry date for sole parent support

para.text: The expiry date for sole parent support for the purposes of sections 331 to 336 of the Act is,—

in the case of a beneficiary receiving sole parent support to which the beneficiary was transferred on 15 July 2013 under clause 2(6) to (8) or (9) and (11) of Schedule 32 of the Social Security Act 1964, the 12-month anniversary of the date on which the beneficiary’s original benefit commenced or, as the case may be, last commenced; and
in any other case, the 12-month anniversary of the date on which the beneficiary’s sole parent support commenced or, as the case may be, last commenced.
For the purposes of subclause (1)﻿(a), the beneficiary’s original benefit means the benefit that the beneficiary was receiving immediately before 15 July 2013 that was either—
a domestic purposes benefit under section 27B of the Social Security Act 1964; or
a widow’s benefit under section 21 of the Social Security Act 1964.

prov.heading: Circumstances in which under section 334 of Act specified benefit does not expire under section 332 of Act

para.text: Each of the following paragraphs prescribes circumstances in which under section 334 of the Act a specified benefit does not expire under section 332 of the Act:
the beneficiary is a patient in hospital care within the meaning of section 4(1) of the Health and Disability Services (Safety) Act 2001:
the beneficiary is in residential disability care or rest home care within the meaning of section 4(1) of the Health and Disability Services (Safety) Act 2001:

the beneficiary resides in, or is present in and cannot reasonably leave, an area in respect of which a state of local emergency or state of national emergency is in force under Part 4 of the Civil Defence Emergency Management Act 2002:

the beneficiary resides in, or is present in and cannot reasonably leave, an area that is or may be affected by an outbreak of a quarantinable disease in respect of which an epidemic notice has been given, and is in force, under section 5(1) of the Epidemic Preparedness Act 2006:
the beneficiary resides in, or is present in and cannot reasonably leave, an area that is or may be affected by any exceptional circumstances other than those prescribed by paragraphs (c) and (d).

history.history-note: Regulation 189: replaced, on 1 July 2025, by section 71 of the Social Security Amendment Act 2025 (2025 No 25).

subpart.crosshead: Requirements for regrant: specified benefits other than jobseeker support

history.history-note: Heading: inserted, on 1 July 2025, by section 72 of the Social Security Amendment Act 2025 (2025 No 25).

prov.heading: Specified benefits to which regulations 190A to 190G apply

para.text: Regulations 190A to 190G apply only to specified benefits other than jobseeker support.
A reference in regulations 190A to 190G to a specified benefit therefore excludes jobseeker support (see regulations 190H to 190P).

history.history-note: Regulation 190: replaced, on 1 July 2025, by section 72 of the Social Security Amendment Act 2025 (2025 No 25).

prov.label: 190A
prov.heading: Requirements for reapplication form, supporting evidence, etc

para.text: A specified benefit must not be regranted to a person to whom section 336 of the Act applies unless—
MSD has received—

a reapplication form (provided by MSD for the purpose) completed to MSD’s satisfaction by or on behalf of the beneficiary and the beneficiary’s spouse or partner who is included in the beneficiary’s specified benefit (if any) (in this regulation, and in regulations 190B to 190G, called any included spouse or partner); and
any supporting evidence reasonably required by MSD; and

the beneficiary and the beneficiary’s work-tested spouse or partner (if they are an included spouse or partner) have participated, to the satisfaction of MSD, in a comprehensive work assessment of a kind specified by MSD.
Subclause (1) is subject to subclause (3) and to regulations 190B to 190G.
For the purposes of this regulation, a failure of a beneficiary’s work-tested spouse or partner to participate in a comprehensive work assessment in accordance with subclause (1)﻿(b)—
must not be treated as a failure to complete the requirements of subclause (1)﻿(a) and (b); but
must be treated as a failure by that spouse or partner to comply with that spouse’s or partner’s work-test obligations.

history.history-note: Regulation 190A: inserted, on 1 July 2025, by section 72 of the Social Security Amendment Act 2025 (2025 No 25).

prov.label: 190B
prov.heading: Requirements completed before expiry date

para.text: This regulation applies only if, in relation to a person, the requirements of regulation 190A(1)﻿(a) and (b) are completed (by the person and by any included spouse or partner) before the expiry date (as defined in section 331(1) of the Act) for the person’s specified benefit.
MSD must regrant the person’s specified benefit if MSD is satisfied that the person meets or continues to meet the eligibility criteria for that benefit.
If regranted, that benefit commences on that expiry date.

history.history-note: Regulation 190B: inserted, on 1 July 2025, by section 72 of the Social Security Amendment Act 2025 (2025 No 25).

prov.label: 190C
prov.heading: Requirements completed in 20 working days starting on expiry date

para.text: This regulation applies if, in relation to a person and any included spouse or partner,—

the requirements of regulation 190A(1)﻿(a) and (b) are completed (by the person and by any included spouse or partner) within 20 working days starting on the expiry date for the person’s specified benefit; and
MSD is satisfied that, at any time or times when or after MSD did the following, exceptional circumstances existed that prevented those requirements from being completed before that expiry date:
gave the beneficiary a notice stating the matters set out in section 335(1)﻿(a) to (c) of the Act, as required by section 335(1) of the Act; or
took reasonable steps to advise the beneficiary of those matters, as required by section 335(2) and (3) of the Act.
MSD must regrant the person’s specified benefit if MSD is satisfied that—
the person meets the eligibility criteria (including, without limitation, the requirements in section 29 of the Act) for that benefit set out in Part 2 of the Act; and
on and after that expiry date, and unless exceptional circumstances prevented the person from doing so, the person continued to meet the eligibility criteria for that benefit.
If regranted, that benefit commences on that expiry date.

If regranted, that benefit is not payable to the person for the relevant period as well as the conditional payment to the person for the relevant period of the following that are payable under regulation 190E:
the specified benefit; and
the other assistance specified in regulation 190E(3)﻿(b).

history.history-note: Regulation 190C: inserted, on 1 July 2025, by section 72 of the Social Security Amendment Act 2025 (2025 No 25).

prov.label: 190D
prov.heading: Person intending to reapply contacts MSD before expiry date

para.text: This regulation applies if a person intending to reapply for a specified benefit, or any included spouse or partner, contacts MSD before the expiry date for the person’s specified benefit.

MSD may set a time (being a time within 10 working days starting on that expiry date) before which the requirements of regulation 190A(1)﻿(a) and (b) are to be completed (by the person and by any included spouse or partner).
If those requirements are completed (by the person and by any included spouse or partner) before that time, MSD may regrant that benefit if MSD is satisfied that—
the person meets the eligibility criteria (including, without limitation, the requirements in section 29 of the Act) for that benefit set out in Part 2 of the Act; and
on and after that expiry date, and unless exceptional circumstances prevented the person from doing so, the person continued to meet the eligibility criteria for that benefit.
If regranted, that benefit commences on that expiry date.

If regranted, that benefit is not payable to the person for the relevant period as well as the conditional payment to the person for the relevant period of the following that are payable under regulation 190E:
the specified benefit; and
the other assistance specified in regulation 190E(3)﻿(b).

history.history-note: Regulation 190D: inserted, on 1 July 2025, by section 72 of the Social Security Amendment Act 2025 (2025 No 25).

prov.label: 190E
prov.heading: Conditional payment of specified benefit and other assistance

para.text: This regulation applies only if, in relation to a person,—
the requirements of regulation 190A(1)﻿(a) and (b) are not completed (by the person and by any included spouse or partner) before the expiry date (as defined in section 331(1) of the Act); and
the person’s specified benefit is not regranted, and does not commence on that expiry date, under regulation 190B(2) and (3); and
those requirements may be completed in the relevant period.
The relevant period, for the purposes of this regulation, is—
the 20-working-day period mentioned in regulation 190C; or
if regulation 190D applies, the period that—
starts when the person, or any included spouse or partner, contacts MSD; and
ends at the time MSD sets under regulation 190D before which the requirements of regulation 190A(1)﻿(a) and (b) are to be completed.
MSD must, for the relevant period, grant and pay the person—
the specified benefit; and
all other assistance under this Act—
payable to the person immediately before the expiry date for the specified benefit; and
that would have continued to be payable to the person had their specified benefit not expired.
Subclause (3) does not limit any review under section 304 of the Act.

Payment of a benefit and assistance granted under this regulation is subject to the condition that the person is liable to repay the whole of any amount paid for the relevant period if the person’s specified benefit is not regranted under regulation 190C or 190D.
Any amount the person is liable to repay under this regulation is a debt due to the Crown and subject to recovery under section 362(1) of the Act (MSD’s duty to recover debts) by MSD from the person.

history.history-note: Regulation 190E: inserted, on 1 July 2025, by section 72 of the Social Security Amendment Act 2025 (2025 No 25).

prov.label: 190F
prov.heading: When reapplication form meets application form requirements

para.text: Regulations 190A to 190D apply for the purposes of section 297 of the Act.

history.history-note: Regulation 190F: inserted, on 1 July 2025, by section 72 of the Social Security Amendment Act 2025 (2025 No 25).

prov.label: 190G
prov.heading: When reapplication form treated as new application form

para.text: This regulation applies to a person if the requirements of regulation 190A(1)﻿(a) and (b)—
are not completed (by the person and by any included spouse or partner) within 20 working days starting on the expiry date for the person’s specified benefit; or

were completed (by the person and by any included spouse or partner) within that 20-working-day period, and MSD is satisfied that there were no exceptional circumstances that prevented those requirements from being completed (by the person and by any included spouse or partner) before that expiry date.
The specified benefit must not be regranted to the person.
Any reapplication form received from or on behalf of that person must be treated as an application form for the purposes of section 297(1) of the Act and regulation 166.

history.history-note: Regulation 190G: inserted, on 1 July 2025, by section 72 of the Social Security Amendment Act 2025 (2025 No 25).

subpart.crosshead: Requirements for regrant: specified benefits that are jobseeker support

history.history-note: Heading: inserted, on 1 July 2025, by section 72 of the Social Security Amendment Act 2025 (2025 No 25).

prov.label: 190H
prov.heading: Specified benefits to which regulations 190J to 190P apply

para.text: Regulations 190J to 190P apply only to jobseeker support.

history.history-note: Regulation 190H: inserted, on 1 July 2025, by section 72 of the Social Security Amendment Act 2025 (2025 No 25).

prov.label: 190I
prov.heading: Meaning of period allowed to complete requirements

prov.body.subprov.crosshead: Definition

para.text: In regulations 190J to 190P, the period allowed to complete the requirements, for a person and any included spouse or partner referred to in regulation 190J(1)﻿(a)﻿(i), means—
for the purposes of the person and any included spouse or partner completing requirements in regulation 190J(1)﻿(a) and (b), the period specified in subclause (2); and
for the purposes of the person and any included spouse or partner completing requirements in regulation 190J(1)﻿(c), the period specified in subclause (3).

prov.body.subprov.crosshead: Period for requirements in regulation 190J(1)﻿(a) and (b)

para.text: The period for requirements in regulation 190J(1)﻿(a) and (b)—

starts when MSD gives the person notice of the matters set out in section 335(1)﻿(a) to (c) of the Act, as required by section 335(1) of the Act, or when MSD takes reasonable steps to advise the person of those matters, as required by section 335(2) and (3) of the Act; and
ends at the close of the day before the expiry date (as defined in section 331(1) of the Act) for the person’s jobseeker support.

prov.body.subprov.crosshead: Period for requirements in regulation 190J(1)﻿(c)

para.text: The period for requirements in regulation 190J(1)﻿(c) is the 13-week period that—
starts on the day that is 13 weeks before the expiry date (as defined in section 331(1) of the Act) for the person’s jobseeker support; and
ends at the close of the day before the expiry date (as defined in section 331(1) of the Act) for the person’s jobseeker support.

history.history-note: Regulation 190I: inserted, on 1 July 2025, by section 72 of the Social Security Amendment Act 2025 (2025 No 25).

prov.label: 190J
prov.heading: Requirements for reapplication form, supporting evidence, etc

prov.body.subprov.crosshead: Requirements

para.text: Jobseeker support must not be regranted to a person unless—
MSD receives within the period allowed to complete the requirements—

a reapplication form (provided by MSD for the purpose) completed to MSD’s satisfaction by the person, and by the person’s spouse or partner who is included in the person’s jobseeker support (if any) (in regulation 190I, in this regulation, and in regulations 190K to 190P, called any included spouse or partner); and
any supporting evidence reasonably required by MSD; and

the person and any included spouse or partner have confirmed to MSD within the period allowed to complete the requirements that they will continue to meet their obligations under Part 3 of the Act; and
MSD is satisfied within the period allowed to complete the requirements that—
information provided by or on behalf of the person and any included spouse or partner shows that the person meets the requirements in section 20(a) and (c) to (e) of the Act; and

the person is available for work (within the meaning of sections 20(b) and 22 of the Act) and has participated, to the satisfaction of MSD, in an engagement that MSD considers suitable for improving their work readiness or prospects for employment; and

any work-tested spouse or partner (if they are an included spouse or partner) has participated, to MSD’s satisfaction, in an engagement that MSD considers suitable for improving their work readiness or prospects for employment.

prov.body.subprov.crosshead: Requirements subject to subclauses (3) to (5) and to regulations 190K to 190P

para.text: Subclause (1) is subject to—
subclauses (3) to (5); and
regulations 190K to 190P.

prov.body.subprov.crosshead: Exception to requirements in subclause (1)﻿(a) to (c)

para.text: Any included spouse or partner is not required to complete the requirements in subclause (1)﻿(a) to (c) if the spouse or partner is in any of the specific circumstances described in regulation 189.

prov.body.subprov.crosshead: Exception to requirements in subclause (1)﻿(c)﻿(ii)

para.text: The requirements in subclause (1)﻿(c)﻿(ii) do not apply to—
a person who holds an exemption granted under section 158(1) of the Act from all of the person’s—
work-preparation obligations; and
work-test obligations; or
a person who is receiving jobseeker support on the ground of a health condition, injury, or disability, and who has—
a job to return to; and
a confirmed return-to-work date.

prov.body.subprov.crosshead: How MSD may be satisfied requirements in subclause (1)﻿(a) to (c) completed

para.text: Requirements in subclause (1)﻿(a) to (c) may be completed at different times within the period allowed to complete the requirements, and MSD may be satisfied that those requirements are completed based on information—
available to MSD during that period; and
related to the circumstances of the person and any included spouse or partner, and to their entitlement to assistance under this Act.

history.history-note: Regulation 190J: inserted, on 1 July 2025, by section 72 of the Social Security Amendment Act 2025 (2025 No 25).

prov.label: 190K
prov.heading: Requirements completed before expiry date

para.text: This regulation applies only if, in relation to a person, the requirements of regulation 190J(1)﻿(a) to (c) are completed (by the person and by any included spouse or partner) within the period allowed to complete the requirements.
MSD must regrant the person’s jobseeker support.
If regranted, that benefit commences on that expiry date.

history.history-note: Regulation 190K: inserted, on 1 July 2025, by section 72 of the Social Security Amendment Act 2025 (2025 No 25).

prov.label: 190L
prov.heading: Requirements completed in 20 working days starting on expiry date

para.text: This regulation applies if, in relation to the person and any included spouse or partner,—

the requirements of regulation 190J(1)﻿(a) to (c) are completed (by the person and by any included spouse or partner) within 20 working days starting on the expiry date for the person’s jobseeker support; and

MSD is satisfied that, at any time or times within the period allowed to complete the requirements, exceptional circumstances existed that prevented those requirements from being completed within that period.
MSD must regrant the person’s jobseeker support if MSD is satisfied that—
the person meets the eligibility criteria for that benefit in section 20 of the Act; and

on and after that expiry date, and unless exceptional circumstances prevented the person from doing so, the person continued to meet the eligibility criteria for that benefit in section 20 of the Act.
If regranted, that benefit commences on that expiry date.

If regranted, that benefit is not payable to the person for the relevant period as well as the conditional payment to the person for the relevant period of the following that are payable under regulation 190N:
jobseeker support; and
the other assistance specified in regulation 190N(3)﻿(b).

history.history-note: Regulation 190L: inserted, on 1 July 2025, by section 72 of the Social Security Amendment Act 2025 (2025 No 25).

prov.label: 190M
prov.heading: Person intending to reapply contacts MSD before expiry date

para.text: This regulation applies if a person intending to reapply for jobseeker support, or any included spouse or partner, contacts MSD before the expiry date for the person’s jobseeker support.

MSD may set a time (being a time within 10 working days starting on that expiry date) before which the requirements of regulation 190J(1)﻿(a) to (c) are to be completed (by the person and by any included spouse or partner).
If those requirements are completed (by the person and by any included spouse or partner) before that time, MSD may regrant the person’s jobseeker support if MSD is satisfied that—
the person meets the eligibility criteria for that benefit in section 20 of the Act; and

on and after that expiry date, and unless exceptional circumstances prevented the person from doing so, the person continued to meet the eligibility criteria for that benefit in section 20 of the Act.
If regranted, that benefit commences on that expiry date.

If regranted, that benefit is not payable to the person for the relevant period as well as the conditional payment to the person for the relevant period of the following that are payable under regulation 190N:
jobseeker support; and
the other assistance specified in regulation 190N(3)﻿(b).

history.history-note: Regulation 190M: inserted, on 1 July 2025, by section 72 of the Social Security Amendment Act 2025 (2025 No 25).

prov.label: 190N
prov.heading: Conditional payment of jobseeker support and other assistance

para.text: This regulation applies only if, in relation to a person,—
the requirements of regulation 190J(1)﻿(a) to (c) are not completed (by the person and by any included spouse or partner) within the period allowed to complete the requirements; and
the person’s jobseeker support is not regranted, and does not commence on that expiry date, under regulation 190K(2) and (3); and
those requirements may be completed in the relevant period.
The relevant period, for the purposes of this regulation, is—
the 20-working-day period mentioned in regulation 190L; or
if regulation 190M applies, the period that—
starts when the person, or any included spouse or partner, contacts MSD; and
ends at the time MSD sets under regulation 190M before which the requirements of regulation 190J(1)﻿(a) to (c) are to be completed.
MSD must, for the relevant period, grant and pay the person—
jobseeker support; and
all other assistance under this Act—
payable to the person immediately before the expiry date for the person’s jobseeker support; and
that would have continued to be payable to the person had their jobseeker support not expired.
Subclause (3) does not limit any review under section 304 of the Act.

Payment of jobseeker support and assistance granted under this regulation is subject to the condition that the person is liable to repay the whole of any amount paid for the relevant period if the person’s jobseeker support is not regranted under regulation 190L or 190M.
Any amount the person is liable to repay under this regulation is a debt due to the Crown and subject to recovery under section 362(1) of the Act (MSD’s duty to recover debts) by MSD from the person.

history.history-note: Regulation 190N: inserted, on 1 July 2025, by section 72 of the Social Security Amendment Act 2025 (2025 No 25).

prov.label: 190O
prov.heading: When reapplication form meets application form requirements

para.text: Regulations 190J to 190M apply for the purposes of section 297 of the Act.

history.history-note: Regulation 190O: inserted, on 1 July 2025, by section 72 of the Social Security Amendment Act 2025 (2025 No 25).

prov.label: 190P
prov.heading: When reapplication form treated as new application form

para.text: This regulation applies to a person if the requirements of regulation 190J(1)﻿(a) to (c)—
are not completed (by the person and by any included spouse or partner) within 20 working days starting on the expiry date for the person’s jobseeker support; or

were completed (by the person and by any included spouse or partner) within that 20-working-day period, and MSD is satisfied that there were no exceptional circumstances that prevented those requirements from being completed (by the person and by any included spouse or partner) within the period allowed to complete the requirements.
Jobseeker support must not be regranted to the person.
Any reapplication form received from or on behalf of the person is treated as an application form for the purposes of section 297(1) of the Act and regulation 166.

history.history-note: Regulation 190P: inserted, on 1 July 2025, by section 72 of the Social Security Amendment Act 2025 (2025 No 25).

subpart.heading: Apportionment and payments on death of beneficiary

prov.heading: Apportionment

para.text: This regulation applies to, and enables MSD to apportion between a beneficiary and the beneficiary’s spouse or partner, the following (other than benefits specified in section 442(3) of the Act):
a benefit payable at a rate prescribed for a person who is in a relationship (a couple rate):
any other benefit payable under the Act.
Benefits to which this regulation applies are payable as follows (unless they are apportioned differently by MSD under subclause (3)):
50% must be paid to the beneficiary; and
50% must be paid to the spouse or partner who qualifies the beneficiary to be paid at the couple rate.
If MSD determines that it would not be appropriate for benefits to which this regulation applies to be paid under subclause (2), they are payable—
wholly to the beneficiary (and so not at all to the spouse or partner who qualifies the beneficiary to be paid at the couple rate), if MSD determines that is appropriate; or
in any other unequal proportions MSD determines are appropriate, to the beneficiary and to the spouse or partner who qualifies the beneficiary to be paid at the couple rate.
When a benefit is apportioned under this regulation, the proportion paid to a person is for the purposes of the Income Tax Act 2007 income of that person.

prov.heading: Payment on death of beneficiary

para.text: This subclause applies if,—
on the death of a beneficiary, an amount of the benefit concerned (which may include an instalment already due that has not been paid and remains payable) is unpaid; and
the amount is not disposed of under section 346 of the Act (for example, to or for the benefit of the surviving spouse or partner, or a dependent child or children, of the beneficiary).
If subclause (1) applies,—

if probate of the beneficiary’s will has been granted, or letters of administration of the beneficiary’s estate have been issued, the amount must be paid to the beneficiary’s executor or administrator:
in any other case, the amount must be paid in accordance with section 65(2) and (3) of the Administration Act 1969.

history.history-note: Subpart 4A: inserted, on 26 May 2025, by section 73 of the Social Security Amendment Act 2025 (2025 No 25).

prov.label: 192A
prov.heading: Required manner of payment: money management for certain payments to people subject to sanction for first failure

prov.body.subprov.crosshead: Application

para.text: This regulation applies—
to a main benefit of a person to whom section 236A applies (P); and
while money management imposed on P has effect.
In particular, this regulation applies to P’s main benefit (without limiting the generality of subclause (1)) while both of the following apply:
P’s main benefit is suspended for reasons unrelated to a sanction for failure to comply with 1 or more obligations under the Act that are specified in section 233 of the Act:
money management imposed on P has effect.

prov.body.subprov.crosshead: General rule

para.text: The prescribed proportion of an instalment of P’s main benefit, and that is an instalment payable to P (see regulation 163A(1)), must be paid by crediting that amount to a payment card for 4 weeks to enable P to obtain goods or services—
that MSD considers are essential; and
from participating suppliers.
A participating supplier, for the purposes of this section, means a supplier who has agreed with MSD to supply goods or services—
that MSD considers are essential; and
to a person on whom money management has been imposed; and
on the person’s presentation of the payment card.

prov.body.subprov.crosshead: Exceptions: arrears that MSD must pay, or debt that MSD must recover

para.text: Arrears that MSD must pay to P, and that are arrears that relate to a period while money management imposed on P has effect (even if the arrears are unrelated to P’s main benefit, but excluding arrears relating to a suspension to which subclause (2) applies), are—
paid to, or on account of, P personally under section 339(1) of the Act; and
not credited to P’s payment card.

Arrears that MSD must pay to P, and that are arrears that relate to a period while money management imposed on P has effect (even if the arrears are unrelated to P’s main benefit), and that are arrears relating to a suspension to which subclause (2) applies, are paid as follows:
the prescribed proportion of them is credited to P’s payment card:
the rest of them is paid to, or on account of, P personally under section 339(1) of the Act.
However, if P dies while money management imposed on P has effect, arrears to which subclause (6) applies must (despite that subclause) be paid under—
section 346 of the Act (required manner of payment: payment on death of beneficiary); or
regulation 192 (payment on death of beneficiary).

Debt that MSD must recover from P, and that is debt that relates to a period while money management imposed on P has effect (even if the debt is unrelated to P’s main benefit, and even if it is debt relating to a suspension to which subclause (2) applies), is recovered—
from, or on account of, P personally; and
not from, or from amounts to be credited to, P’s payment card.

prov.body.subprov.crosshead: Exception: if P dies while money management has effect

para.text: However, if P dies while money management imposed on P has effect, the prescribed proportion of an instalment of P’s main benefit that is an instalment payable to P must (despite the rest of this regulation) be paid under—
section 346 of the Act (required manner of payment: payment on death of beneficiary); or
regulation 192 (payment on death of beneficiary).

history.history-note: Regulation 192A: inserted, on 26 May 2025, by section 73 of the Social Security Amendment Act 2025 (2025 No 25).
Subpart 5 heading: amended, on 26 May 2025, by section 75 of the Social Security Amendment Act 2025 (2025 No 25).

prov.heading: When youth money management applies

para.text: Regulation 194 applies to a young person (subject to subclause (2)) if—
a specified payment (as defined in subclause (3)) is payable to a young person; and
the young person has given to MSD or to the young person’s contracted service provider the information required under all or any of section 162(1)﻿(i) of the Act and regulations 166 and 173; and
the young person has not opted out under regulation 196.

Until the information required under all or any of section 162(1)﻿(i) of the Act and regulations 166 and 173 has been given, a payment referred to in subclause (1)﻿(a) must be paid to or on account of the young person entitled to it in accordance with section 339(1) of the Act.
In this subpart, specified payment, in relation to a young person, means,—
in accordance with section 341(3)﻿(a) to (c) of the Act,—
any youth support payment (including the in-hand allowance up to the maximum amount set out in clause 7 of Part 6 of Schedule 4 of the Act):
any WFF tax credit payable to a young person by MSD:
the amount of any child disability allowance or disability allowance to which a young person is entitled:
for the purposes of section 341(3)﻿(d) of the Act,—

the amount of any accommodation supplement, temporary additional support, or childcare assistance to which the young person is entitled (other than a payment for childcare assistance made to a provider under subpart 6 (childcare assistance) of Part 2 (assistance) of these regulations):

the amount of any special assistance of an ongoing kind under section 101 of the Act to which the young person is entitled (other than a payment for childcare made to a provider under any such welfare programme):
the amount of any incentive payment (as defined in Schedule 2 of the Act) payable to the young person:
in relation to a young person to whom section 166(1) or 167(1) of the Act applies, the portion of the specified benefit payable to the young person under regulation 191 (apportionment):
the amount of any winter energy payment payable to the young person.

history.history-note: Regulation 193 heading: amended, on 26 May 2025, by section 75 of the Social Security Amendment Act 2025 (2025 No 25).

prov.heading: How specified payment may be paid

para.text: MSD must pay each instalment of a specified payment payable to the young person in the following order and manner:
first, by paying such amount as MSD thinks reasonable in the circumstances of the young person directly towards the young person’s accommodation costs and service costs:

secondly, by paying such amount as MSD thinks reasonable in the circumstances of the young person directly towards the young person’s debts or other liabilities (including any reduction or deduction required or authorised under the Act):
thirdly, by paying to or on account of the young person the in-hand allowance up to the amount specified in clause 7 of Part 6 of Schedule 4 of the Act:

fourthly, unless subclause (2) applies, by crediting such amount as MSD thinks reasonable in the circumstances of the young person to a payment card, voucher, or device that enables the young person to obtain goods or services from a participating supplier.
Subclause (1) is subject to regulation 195 and section 282(3)﻿(c) of the Act.
In the case of the supply of goods or services to a young person by a non-participating supplier, MSD may pay the amount that would have been paid under subclause (1)﻿(d) either—
directly to that supplier; or
to or on account of the young person.

In this regulation, participating supplier means a supplier who has agreed with the department (as defined in section 3(1) of the Social Security Act 1964) or MSD to supply goods or services to young persons on presentation of a payment card, voucher, or other device, and non-participating supplier means a supplier who has not so agreed.

history.history-note: Regulation 194(2): amended, on 17 November 2025, by regulation 6 of the Social Security (Youth Money Management) Amendment Regulations 2025 (SL 2025/223).

prov.heading: Payments that may be paid to young person or on account of young person

para.text: MSD may pay all or part of the following payments to or on account of a young person to whom regulation 194 applies:
any disability allowance or child disability allowance:
any special assistance under section 101(1) of the Act that is not of an ongoing kind:
any payments under the course participation assistance programme established under section 101(1) of the Act:
any flexible childcare assistance under the Employment and Work Readiness Assistance Programme:
any payment under the Training Incentive Allowance Programme:
any advance payment of all or part of 1 or more instalments of a benefit under section 347 of the Act.
Subclause (1) is subject to subclause (3).
MSD may only make a payment under subclause (1)﻿(b) to (d) after first consulting the young person’s contracted service provider, if there is one.

history.history-note: Regulation 195 heading: amended, on 17 November 2025, by regulation 7(1) of the Social Security (Youth Money Management) Amendment Regulations 2025 (SL 2025/223).
Regulation 195(1)﻿(c): amended, on 17 November 2025, by regulation 7(2)﻿(a) of the Social Security (Youth Money Management) Amendment Regulations 2025 (SL 2025/223).
Regulation 195(1)﻿(c): amended, on 17 November 2025, by regulation 7(2)﻿(b) of the Social Security (Youth Money Management) Amendment Regulations 2025 (SL 2025/223).
Regulation 195(1)﻿(ca): inserted, on 17 November 2025, by regulation 7(3) of the Social Security (Youth Money Management) Amendment Regulations 2025 (SL 2025/223).
Regulation 195(1)﻿(cb): inserted, on 17 November 2025, by regulation 7(3) of the Social Security (Youth Money Management) Amendment Regulations 2025 (SL 2025/223).

prov.heading: When young person may opt out of youth money management

para.text: A young person receiving a youth payment may opt out of youth money management if—
the young person is 18 years or older; and
the payment is being continued under section 54(3) of the Act.
A young person receiving a young parent payment may opt out of youth money management if—
the young person is 20 years or older; and
the payment is being continued under section 61(3) of the Act.
A young person opts out of youth money management by giving MSD written notice that the young person opts out of youth money management.
Regulation 194 does not apply to a young person who has opted out of youth money management under this regulation.

history.history-note: Regulation 196 heading: amended, on 26 May 2025, by section 75 of the Social Security Amendment Act 2025 (2025 No 25).
Regulation 196(1): amended, on 26 May 2025, by section 75 of the Social Security Amendment Act 2025 (2025 No 25).
Regulation 196(2): amended, on 26 May 2025, by section 75 of the Social Security Amendment Act 2025 (2025 No 25).
Regulation 196(3): amended, on 26 May 2025, by section 75 of the Social Security Amendment Act 2025 (2025 No 25).
Regulation 196(4): amended, on 26 May 2025, by section 75 of the Social Security Amendment Act 2025 (2025 No 25).

prov.heading: Criteria for managing payments

para.text: The criteria referred to in section 342 of the Act for the management of a young person’s own payments are as follows:
the young person has demonstrated continued compliance with the young person’s obligations under sections 162 to 164 of the Act; and

the young person’s contracted service provider has advised MSD that it considers the young person to be financially competent or, as the case requires, MSD considers the young person to be financially competent.

prov.heading: Young person who opts out of youth money management or meets criteria for managing own payments may request payments redirected

para.text: MSD, on the request of a young person who has opted out of youth money management under regulation 196 or has met the criteria set out in regulation 197 for managing the young person’s own payments, may pay all or part of a specified payment payable to the young person directly towards—
the young person’s accommodation and service costs:
the young person’s lawful debts or other liabilities (including any reduction or deduction required or authorised under the Act).
A request under subclause (1)—
must be in writing; and
must specify the costs, debts, or other liabilities towards which MSD must pay the payment; and
may be withdrawn or otherwise cancelled at any time.

history.history-note: Regulation 198 heading: amended, on 26 May 2025, by section 75 of the Social Security Amendment Act 2025 (2025 No 25).
Regulation 198(1): amended, on 26 May 2025, by section 75 of the Social Security Amendment Act 2025 (2025 No 25).

prov.heading: Electing youth money management

para.text: A young person to whom section 166 or 167 of the Act applies for the time being may elect youth money management by giving MSD written notice that the young person elects youth money management.

history.history-note: Regulation 199 heading: amended, on 26 May 2025, by section 75 of the Social Security Amendment Act 2025 (2025 No 25).
Regulation 199: amended, on 26 May 2025, by section 75 of the Social Security Amendment Act 2025 (2025 No 25).

prov.heading: Payment of specified payments if young person has elected youth money management

para.text: MSD must pay each instalment of a specified payment payable to a young person who has elected youth money management under regulation 199 in the following order and manner:
first, by paying such amount as MSD thinks reasonable in the circumstances of the young person directly towards the young person’s accommodation costs and service costs:

secondly, by paying such amount as MSD thinks reasonable in the circumstances of the young person directly towards the young person’s debts or other liabilities (including any reduction or deduction required or authorised under the Act):
thirdly, by paying the balance (if any, after payments under paragraphs (a) and (b)) to or on account of the young person.

history.history-note: Regulation 200 heading: amended, on 26 May 2025, by section 75 of the Social Security Amendment Act 2025 (2025 No 25).
Regulation 200: amended, on 26 May 2025, by section 75 of the Social Security Amendment Act 2025 (2025 No 25).

prov.heading: Interpretation

para.text: In this subpart, unless the context otherwise requires,—
applicable payment means any of the following:
an advance payment of all or part of 1 or more instalments of a benefit under section 347 of the Act:
a payment under the Special Needs Grants Programme (excluding Parts 5, 6, and 6A) established and approved on 18 December 1998 (and continued under clause 21 of Schedule 1 of the Act):
a payment under the Recoverable Assistance Programme (excluding Part 3A) established and approved on 15 February 1999 (and continued under clause 21 of Schedule 1 of the Act)
applicant means—
a beneficiary who wishes to make an application:
a beneficiary by whom or on whose behalf an application is made
application means an application for an advance payment of all or part of 1 or more instalments of a benefit under section 347 of the Act
budgeting activity means a budgeting activity of a kind prescribed in regulation 204.

prov.heading: Requirements for application

para.text: An applicant and the applicant’s spouse or partner (if any) must,—
if required by MSD, complete an application form provided by MSD; and
provide any information, or give an answer to any question, that MSD reasonably requires to determine the applicant’s application.
MSD may require an applicant and the applicant’s spouse or partner (if any) to comply with subclause (1)﻿(b)—
in the manner specified by MSD; and
whether or not they are required to complete the application form referred to in subclause (1)﻿(a).

prov.heading: Circumstances when budgeting activity may be required

para.text: For the purposes of section 348 of the Act, the circumstances in which an applicant or the applicant’s spouse or partner (if any), or both, may be required to undertake a budgeting activity are as follows:
if the applicant has received 3 or more applicable payments within—
the period that starts on the date of commencement of these regulations and ends on the date on which the application is made, if that period is less than 12 months; or
the preceding period of 12 months that ends on the date on which the application is made, if that date is 12 months or more after the date of commencement of these regulations; or

if paragraph (a) does not apply to the applicant, but MSD, after taking into account the following matters, considers that it would be beneficial for the applicant or the applicant’s spouse or partner (if any), or both, to undertake a budgeting activity to better manage their finances:
the applicant has received repeated applicable payments for the same cost (whether the payments were received before or after the date of commencement of these regulations):
the applicant’s history of receiving multiple applicable payments (whether that history relates to a period before or after the date of commencement of these regulations):

the amount of the applicant’s debts that are due to the Crown (within the meaning of section 353 of the Act) and whether that amount is materially significant to the management of the applicant’s finances.
If 1 or more applicable payments are made to the applicant on the same day, those payments are to be counted as 1 applicable payment under this regulation.

prov.heading: Kinds of budgeting activities

para.text: For the purposes of section 348 of the Act, the kinds of budgeting activities that MSD may require an applicant or the applicant’s spouse or partner (if any), or both, to undertake are as follows:
completing a budgeting work sheet or a work sheet approved by MSD that sets out income against expenditure:
attending a budgeting course or seminar approved by MSD that teaches how to prepare a budget or to improve budgeting skills:
seeking assistance from a recognised budgeting advice service.

prov.heading: Appropriate number of weeks for calculating weekly income

para.text: For the purposes of the definition of appropriate number of weeks in clause 11 of Part 3 of Schedule 3 of the Act and a benefit, the number of weeks is the appropriate number of weeks specified for that benefit in Schedule 6 of these regulations.

Subclause (1) does not prevent MSD, with the agreement of a person whose income would otherwise be determined (under clause 13 of Part 3 of Schedule 3 of the Act) over 52 weeks, determining that person’s income over the period of 1 week.
An agreement with a person under subclause (2) may be ended by the person or by MSD at any time by notice to the other.

prov.heading: Debts due to the Crown

para.text: The following sums are debts due to the Crown:
a penalty payable under the Act:
A penalty payable under section 354 of the Act (recovery of penalty from beneficiary who obtains by fraud amount in excess of entitlement).
A penalty payable under regulation 226 (payer penalty for late deductions).
a benefit paid conditionally or provisionally under the Act that a person has (by direction of MSD or otherwise) become liable to repay:
An amount directed to be repayable under section 228(2) of the Act (MSD’s options in case of misconduct).

An amount the person granted a benefit is liable to repay under section 64 of the Act (emergency benefit: grant during epidemic in New Zealand) and to which this paragraph applies in accordance with section 64(3) of the Act.
An amount recoverable under section 186 of the Act (benefit may be subject to repayment of insurance payment).
An amount recoverable under regulation 184 (delayed redundancy and retirement payments).
An amount recoverable under regulation 185 (seasonal workers made redundant after benefit commences).
An amount recoverable under regulation 190E(6) (conditional payment of specified benefit and other assistance).
An amount recoverable under regulation 190N(6) (conditional payment of jobseeker support and other assistance).
An amount the person participating in approved activities is liable to repay under section 324 of the Act (effect of participation in certain activities on non-entitlement period).
An amount repayable under clause 8 of Part 4 or 5 of Schedule 4 of the Act (holiday allowance paid before 25 December to a person not entitled to it on 25 December).
An amount repayable under clause 13 of Part 4 or 5 of Schedule 4 of the Act (birthday allowance paid before the entitlement date to a person not entitled to it on that date).
an advance payment of all or part of 1 or more instalments of a benefit made to a person under section 347 of the Act:
any money paid to or for the credit of a person as a grant of special assistance under a programme approved under section 101 of the Act that is—
paid as a recoverable grant of assistance; or
otherwise recoverable from that person under the terms and conditions of the programme:
any amount described by the Act or these regulations as a debt due to the Crown from the person:
An amount recoverable under section 186 of the Act (benefit may be subject to repayment of insurance payment).
An amount recoverable under section 197(4)﻿(b) and (c) of the Act (effect of compensation or damages on application for benefit).
An amount recoverable under section 358 of the Act (recovery from spouse or partner who misleads MSD of excess amount beneficiary obtained).

a sum that is an overpayment, because the sum is paid or advanced under the Act, Part 6 of the Veterans’ Support Act 2014, Part 1 of the New Zealand Superannuation and Retirement Income Act 2001, or the Residential Care and Disability Support Services Act 2018, to or for the credit of a person, and the sum is one—
that is in excess of the amount to which the person is entitled; or
to which the person has no entitlement:
An amount recoverable under regulation 222 (deductions are held in trust).
An amount recoverable under regulation 226 (payer penalty for late deductions).
a debt that, under section 260(2), 266(2), or 384(2) of the Act, section 128(a) of the Public and Community Housing Management Act 1992, or any other enactment,—
may be recovered by MSD under regulations made under section 444 of the Act as if it were a debt due to the Crown; or
is a debt due to the Crown for the purposes of, or must be treated as if it were a debt due to the Crown under, such regulations.

history.history-note: Regulation 206(b) example: amended, on 1 July 2025, by section 74 of the Social Security Amendment Act 2025 (2025 No 25).
Regulation 206(b) example: amended, on 1 July 2021, by section 11 of the Social Security (Financial Assistance for Caregivers) Amendment Act 2021 (2021 No 25).
Regulation 206(g): amended, on 1 October 2019, by section 33 of the Kāinga Ora–Homes and Communities Act 2019 (2019 No 50).

prov.heading: Exceptions to duty to recover debts

para.text: MSD’s duty under section 362 of the Act to recover debts is subject to—
regulation 208 (debts caused wholly or partly by errors to which debtors did not intentionally contribute):
regulation 208A (debts caused by disruption to automated electronic system):
regulation 208B (debts caused by specified errors identified through information share child support payment reviews):
subpart 11 (debt recovery suspension) of this Part, or any other regulations (providing for remittance or suspension of debt) made under section 444 of the Act:
a determination (about debts that it is uneconomic to recover) made under subclause (2):
an authorisation (about debts that are to be written off) of the kind specified in subclause (3).

MSD may, for the purposes of subclause (1)﻿(c), make a written determination specifying any 1 or more debts, or any 1 or more classes, descriptions, or kinds of debts, that it is uneconomic to recover.
An authorisation referred to in subclause (1)﻿(d) is a written authorisation (for example, a delegation)—
given jointly by the Minister and by the Minister of Finance for public finance purposes generally; and
that specifies any 1 or more classes, descriptions, or kinds of debts as debts that are to be written off.

history.history-note: Regulation 207(1)﻿(aa): inserted, on 1 July 2023, by section 55 of the Child Support (Pass On) Acts Amendment Act 2023 (2023 No 27).
Regulation 207(1)﻿(ab): inserted, on 1 July 2023, by section 55 of the Child Support (Pass On) Acts Amendment Act 2023 (2023 No 27).

prov.heading: Debts caused wholly or partly by errors to which debtors did not intentionally contribute

para.text: MSD cannot recover under the Act a sum comprising that part of a debt that was caused wholly or partly by an error to which the debtor did not intentionally contribute if—
the debtor—
received that sum in good faith; and
changed the debtor’s position in the belief that the debtor was entitled to that sum and would not have to pay or repay that sum to MSD; and
it would be inequitable in all the circumstances, including the debtor’s financial circumstances, to permit recovery.
In this regulation, error—
means—
the provision of incorrect information by MSD:
any erroneous act or omission of MSD that occurs during an inquiry under section 298 of the Act:
any other erroneous act or omission of MSD; but
does not include the simple act of making a payment to which the recipient is not entitled if that act is not caused, wholly or partly, by any erroneous act or omission of MSD.
In subclause (2), MSD includes a contracted service provider whose action or inaction is under section 377(c) of the Act treated as if it were action or inaction by MSD.

prov.label: 208A
prov.heading: Debts caused by disruption to automated electronic system

para.text: MSD cannot recover under the Act a sum comprising a debt, or that part of a debt, that was caused wholly or partly by a disruption to an automated electronic system.
In this regulation,—
automated electronic system means an automated electronic system used under subpart 5A of Part 6 of the Act
disruption, to an automated electronic system, includes, without limitation, a disruption to disclosure to MSD of all or any information used in or by the system.

history.history-note: Regulation 208A: inserted, on 1 July 2023, by section 56 of the Child Support (Pass On) Acts Amendment Act 2023 (2023 No 27).

prov.label: 208B
prov.heading: Debts caused by specified errors identified through information share child support payment reviews

para.text: MSD cannot recover under the Act a sum comprising a debt, or that part of a debt, that was caused wholly or partly by a specified error identified through an information share child support payment review.
In this regulation,—

information share child support payment review means a review under section 304(1A) of the Act of whether a person’s information share child support payment is or was all or any of their weekly income
specified error means 1 or both of the following:
MSD has charged an amount of income that is not the correct amount (see section 304A(1)﻿(c) of the Act):

a person has been incorrectly not identified as the proper recipient of the payment (whether that non-identification was by an automated electronic system or a person) (see section 304A(1)﻿(d) of the Act).

history.history-note: Regulation 208B: inserted, on 1 July 2023, by section 56 of the Child Support (Pass On) Acts Amendment Act 2023 (2023 No 27).

prov.heading: MSD must determine rate and methods of debt recovery

para.text: MSD must, in performing its duty under section 362 of the Act to recover a debt, determine from time to time—
the rate of recovery; and
the method or methods of recovery to be used.
The methods of recovery that MSD may determine under subclause (1)﻿(b) are to be used are any of the following:
bring proceedings in the name of the chief executive:
deduct all or part of that debt from any amount payable to that person by MSD as a benefit or a student allowance:

for a debt referred to in regulation 206(d) (recoverable special assistance money under an approved welfare programme), deduct all or part of that debt from any payment of a grant of special assistance under a programme approved under section 101 of the Act:
issue a deduction notice under regulation 217.
MSD must, in making the determinations required by subclause (1), have regard to—
relevant considerations specified in debt recovery directions given under regulation 211; and
all other relevant considerations.

Despite subclauses (1)﻿(a) and (3), the rate of recovery of a debt that is an emergency housing contribution must be one that is not inconsistent with the provisions of the Emergency Housing Grants Programme (as those terms are defined in regulation 3(1)) about recovery of a debt of that kind.

history.history-note: Regulation 209(4): inserted, on 30 March 2020, by regulation 6 of the Social Security (Emergency Housing and Transitional Housing) Amendment Regulations 2020 (LI 2020/20).
Regulation 209(4): amended, on 26 August 2024, by regulation 5 of the Social Security (Emergency Housing) Amendment Regulations 2024 (SL 2024/137).

prov.heading: MSD may defer temporarily recovery of debt

para.text: This regulation applies to MSD when it is performing its duty under section 362 of the Act to recover a debt.
MSD may defer temporarily recovery of the debt if MSD is satisfied that, in the particular case, there exist circumstances of a kind specified by debt recovery directions given under regulation 211.
However, nothing in this regulation enables MSD to defer temporarily recovery of a debt that is an emergency housing contribution.

history.history-note: Regulation 210(3): inserted, on 30 March 2020, by regulation 7 of the Social Security (Emergency Housing and Transitional Housing) Amendment Regulations 2020 (LI 2020/20).

prov.heading: Debt recovery directions

para.text: The Minister must give to MSD, and ensure that there are in force (as from time to time amended or replaced) at all times on and after 26 November 2018, debt recovery directions for the purposes of regulations 209 and 210.
Debt recovery directions given under this regulation may, without limitation,—

specify for regulation 209 considerations that differ, or that contemplate different rates of recovery, or methods of recovery, or both, for determinations in respect of different kinds of debts referred to in regulation 206:
specify for regulation 210 different kinds of circumstances for different kinds of debts referred to in regulation 206.
Debt recovery directions given under this regulation are secondary legislation (see Part 3 of the Legislation Act 2019 for publication requirements).

legtable.summary: The following table is small in size and has 3 columns that are grouped under the heading Legislation Act 2019 requirements for secondary legislation made under this regulation.

entry.emphasis: Legislation Act 2019 requirements for secondary legislation made under this regulation

row.entry: The maker must:
• notify it in the Gazette
• publish it on a website maintained by, or on behalf of, the MSD
• if it is amended, publish on that website an updated version of it incorporating the amendments
It is not required to be presented to the House of Representatives because a transitional exemption applies under Schedule 1 of the Legislation Act 2019
It may be disallowed by the House of Representatives

entry.emphasis: This note is not part of the secondary legislation.

history.history-note: Regulation 211(3): replaced, on 28 October 2021, by regulation 121 of the Legislation Act (Sub-delegated Secondary Legislation) Regulations 2021 (LI 2021/248).

prov.heading: Recovery from estate of deceased beneficiary of excess amount beneficiary obtained

para.text: This regulation applies to the estate of a deceased beneficiary (A) if, on or after A’s death, MSD finds A to have been disqualified for any reason from obtaining the benefit, or a benefit of the amount, granted to A.
A is disqualified because A received income, possessed property, or both.

An amount, to be assessed by MSD, equal to the total amount paid to A in excess of the amount (if any) to which A was by law entitled is a debt due to the Crown from, and may be recovered in proceedings brought by or on behalf of the chief executive against, A’s estate.
Recovery of the amount is subject to regulation 208 (but to nothing else in regulations 207 to 211).

prov.heading: Recovery from estate of beneficiary’s deceased spouse or partner of excess amount beneficiary obtained

para.text: This regulation applies to the estate of a deceased spouse or partner (B) of a beneficiary (A) if, on or after B’s death, MSD finds A to have been granted for any reason a benefit, or a benefit of an amount, to which A was not by law entitled.
A was granted a benefit, or a benefit of an amount, to which A was not entitled because B, during B’s lifetime, received income, possessed property, or both.

An amount, to be assessed by MSD, equal to the total amount paid to A in excess of the amount (if any) to which A was by law entitled is a debt due to the Crown from, and may be recovered in proceedings brought by or on behalf of the chief executive against, B’s estate.
Recovery of the amount is subject to regulation 208 (but to nothing else in regulations 207 to 211).

prov.heading: Making and notice of assessment of amount

para.text: In determining under regulation 212 or 213 the total amount paid to A by way of benefit in excess of the amount (if any) to which A was by law entitled, any property that, when A or B died, was in A’s or B’s possession must be taken to have been in A’s or B’s possession on a date that MSD determines and that is, or is after, the date on which the benefit was first granted.
Subclause (1) is overridden to the extent that the contrary is—
proved in proceedings for the recovery of that excess amount; or
decided by the District Court on an application under regulation 215(1).
Written notice of an assessment under regulation 212 or 213 must be served on the executor or administrator of the estate.

prov.heading: Review of assessment of amount

para.text: An executor or administrator of an estate on whom a notice is served under regulation 214(3) may, within 1 month after the service, apply to the District Court to review 1 or both of the following:
the assessment under regulation 212 or 213:
any decision of MSD under regulation 214(1) in relation to the assessment.
The District Court has jurisdiction in respect of the application irrespective of the amount of the assessment.
The application must not be heard in open court.

The court may receive as evidence any statement, document, information, or matter that may in its opinion assist the court to deal effectively with the application, whether or not the same would be otherwise admissible in a court of law.

In determining the application, the court must accept a date determined under regulation 214(1) by MSD as the date on which any property was in the possession of the deceased, unless the court is satisfied from the evidence produced that some other date should be fixed.
In determining the application, the court may—
confirm or cancel, or increase or reduce the amount of, the assessment; or
make any order it considers just and equitable in the circumstances.

prov.heading: Amount due, etc, defined

para.text: In this subpart,—
amount due, for a person, means a debt—
that is an eligible debt due to the Crown, and that is subject to recovery or that may be recovered, from the person; and
notice of which is delivered or posted by MSD to (whether or not the notice is received by) the person; and
that is not paid by the end of the tenth day after the day on which MSD delivers or posts to the person the notice of the debt
debtor means the person (or person’s estate) from whom the amount due may be recovered as a debt due to the Crown
debtor’s payer has the meaning given to it by Schedule 2 of the Act
eligible debt due to the Crown, for a person, means a debt that—
is due to the Crown under regulation 206, and subject to recovery under section 362(1) of the Act, from the person; or
is recoverable, from the person’s estate, under regulation 212(2) (recovery from estate of deceased beneficiary of excess amount beneficiary obtained); or
is recoverable, from the person’s estate, under regulation 213(2) (recovery from estate of beneficiary’s deceased spouse or partner of excess amount beneficiary obtained); or
may be recovered from the person as a debt due to the Crown under section 27X of the Social Security Act 1964 (as that section is saved by section 256(1) of the Child Support Act 1991); or

is an amount of maintenance debt within the meaning of section 61CA of the Social Security Act 1964 (as that section is saved by clause 49 of Schedule 1 of the Act) that may be recovered from the person
salary or wages includes commission, and any other remuneration from employment.

prov.heading: Deduction notice requiring debtor’s payer to deduct, and pay MSD, amount due

para.text: MSD may issue to a debtor’s payer a written deduction notice that requires the debtor’s payer to—
deduct the amount due (as a lump sum, or by instalments) from a sum that is or becomes payable by the debtor’s payer to the debtor; and
pay to MSD by a time, or by any times, specified in the notice, the amount or amounts deducted.
MSD may issue a written deduction notice to the Accident Compensation Corporation that requires the Corporation to—

deduct the amount due from any weekly compensation in respect of loss of earnings or loss of potential earning capacity that is payable, or becomes payable, to the debtor by the Corporation under the Accident Compensation Act 2001; and
pay to MSD at a time, or at any times, specified in the notice the amount or amounts deducted.
MSD cannot issue a deduction notice unless MSD has first taken other reasonably practicable steps (but this subclause does not require MSD to take court or tribunal proceedings) to recover the debt.
Every deduction notice operates subject to regulations 224 (protected earnings), 225 (variation or discharge), and 226 (payer penalty for late deductions).

prov.heading: Contents, copies, and revocation of deduction notice

para.text: MSD must specify in the deduction notice—
whether the deduction is to be made as a lump sum or by instalments; and
the time or times by which the debtor’s payer to whom the deduction notice is issued must pay to MSD the amounts deducted; and
the date, on or after the date on which the deduction notice is issued, on which the deduction notice takes effect.
MSD must issue a copy of the deduction notice to the debtor at the debtor’s last known place of residence or business.
MSD may at any time revoke a deduction notice issued to a debtor’s payer by giving written notice, or by issuing a new deduction notice, to the debtor’s payer.
MSD must revoke a deduction notice if satisfied, on a request for the purpose made by or on behalf of the debtor, that the amount due has been paid.

prov.heading: Debtor’s payer must, if requested, issue debtor deductions statement

para.text: Every debtor’s payer to whom a deduction notice is issued must, if requested by or on behalf of the debtor, issue to the debtor a written statement of—
each amount deducted; and
the purpose or purposes for which the debtor’s payer deducted each amount.

prov.heading: Debtor is, so far as amounts are deducted, discharged from debt

para.text: This regulation applies if a debtor’s payer to whom a deduction notice has been issued deducts, under the notice, any money payable to a debtor.
The debtor is, to the extent of the amount deducted, discharged from the debtor’s debt due to the Crown.

prov.heading: Deduction notices issued to banks

para.text: If the debtor’s payer to whom the deduction notice is issued is a bank, any money held by the bank to the credit of the debtor is subject to regulation 217, and the amount required to be deducted under the deduction notice is, without prejudice to any other remedies against the debtor or any other person,—
deemed to be held in trust for the Crown; and
recoverable from the bank under regulation 206(f) and section 362 of the Act as if it were money payable under a benefit to which the debtor was not entitled.
In this regulation, bank means any of the following:
a registered bank (within the meaning of the Banking (Prudential Supervision) Act 1989):
any other person carrying on in New Zealand the business of banking:
a private savings bank:
a credit union within the meaning of the Friendly Societies and Credit Unions Act 1982:
a building society within the meaning of the Building Societies Act 1965:
the Reserve Bank of New Zealand continued under the Reserve Bank of New Zealand Act 2021, but only in relation to an account maintained by that bank for an employee of that bank.
In this regulation, money held by the bank to the credit of the debtor includes interest on any money that is on deposit or deposited with a bank to the credit of the debtor, whether or not—
the deposit or depositing is on current account:
the money is to be at interest at a fixed term or without limitation of time:
the debtor has made any application to withdraw or uplift the money.
Money on deposit with a bank is, for the purposes of this regulation, taken to be held by the bank to the credit of the debtor if the money—
is held in a joint bank account in the name of the debtor and 1 or more other persons; and
can be withdrawn from the account by or on behalf of the debtor without a signature being required at the time of that withdrawal from, or on behalf of, the other person or persons.

history.history-note: Regulation 221(2)﻿(a): amended, on 1 July 2022, by section 300(2) of the Reserve Bank of New Zealand Act 2021 (2021 No 31).
Regulation 221(2)﻿(f): amended, on 1 July 2022, by section 300(2) of the Reserve Bank of New Zealand Act 2021 (2021 No 31).

prov.heading: Deductions are held in trust

para.text: A debtor’s payer who makes a deduction under a deduction notice is taken to be acting—
on the authority of the debtor and of any other person concerned; and
on behalf of the Crown.
Neither the debtor, nor any other person concerned, has a claim against the debtor’s payer making the deduction, or the Crown, in respect of that deduction.

Any amounts deducted must be held in trust for the Crown, and are debts due to the Crown under regulation 206(f), and subject to recovery under section 362 of the Act, from the debtor’s payer who makes the deductions, as if the amounts were money payable under a benefit to which the debtor’s payer who made the deduction was not entitled.
Any holding and recovery under subclause (3) is without prejudice to any other remedies against the debtor or any other person.

prov.heading: Offences in relation to deduction notices

para.text: A person commits an offence, and is liable on conviction to a fine not exceeding $2,000, if the person—
fails to make any deduction required by a deduction notice; or
fails, after making a deduction, to pay the amount deducted to MSD within the time specified in the notice; or
permits payment to or on behalf of any person, other than MSD, of any amount held in trust for the Crown under regulation 221 or 222.

prov.heading: Protected earnings
