It is not a breach of subclause (1) or (2) for a cardholder to lend the cardholder’s SuperGold Card temporarily to another person for the purpose only of obtaining a service, discount, or concession for the cardholder.

history.history-note: Regulation 291(4A): inserted, on 1 July 2025, by regulation 6 of the Social Security Amendment Regulations (No 2) 2025 (SL 2025/121).
Regulation 291(5): replaced, on 10 October 2022, by regulation 6 of the Social Security Amendment Regulations (No 2) 2022 (SL 2022/251).

prov.heading: Applications and notices

para.text: An application for the issue of a SuperGold Card must be in a form provided by MSD for the purpose.

MSD may require an applicant for a SuperGold Card, or the applicant’s spouse or partner, or both, to supply any information MSD reasonably considers necessary to determine the applicant’s eligibility for the card.

MSD may require any SuperGold Card holder, or the cardholder’s spouse or partner, or both, to supply any relevant information, or answer any relevant questions, that MSD reasonably considers necessary to determine—
whether the cardholder was eligible for the card when it was issued; or
the cardholder’s continued eligibility for the card.
Subclause (5) applies to—
an applicant for a SuperGold Card and the applicant’s spouse or partner; or
a SuperGold Card holder and the cardholder’s spouse or partner.
If a person to whom this subclause applies fails to comply with a requirement under subclause (2) or (3) within a reasonable period specified by MSD, MSD—
may refuse to issue a SuperGold Card to the applicant or (as the case requires) the applicant’s spouse or partner:
may—
refuse to reissue a SuperGold Card to the cardholder or (as the case requires) the cardholder’s spouse or partner; or
recall any SuperGold Card issued to the cardholder or (as the case requires) the cardholder’s spouse or partner.
Every notice required by this subpart must be in writing and must be given to or served on the person to whom it is addressed either personally or—
by leaving it at that person’s place of residence or business; or
by sending it by prepaid letter addressed to that person at one of those places.

In the absence of proof to the contrary, a notice sent by prepaid letter is deemed to have been served when it would have been delivered in the ordinary course of the post and, in proving service, it is enough to prove that the letter was properly addressed and posted.

prov.heading: Offences

para.text: The holder of a SuperGold Card who fails or refuses to do anything required by regulation 289 commits an offence against this regulation.
A person who does anything prohibited by regulation 291 commits an offence against this regulation.
A person who commits an offence against this regulation is liable on conviction to a fine not exceeding $1,000.

prov.heading: Income exemptions

para.text: A person’s income, for the purposes of the Act, and under clause 9 of Schedule 3 of the Act, excludes any amount, item, payment, or income from a specified source that is declared by Schedule 8 of these regulations not to be income.

prov.cf: 1964 No 136 s 3(1), definition of income, paragraph (f)﻿(xvii)

subpart.label: 5A
subpart.heading: Additional general provisions child support payments

history.history-note: Subpart 5A: inserted, on 22 August 2023, by regulation 5 of the Social Security (Additional General Provisions Child Support Payments) Amendment Regulations 2023 (SL 2023/188).

subpart.crosshead: Purpose and effect

history.history-note: Heading: inserted, on 22 August 2023, by regulation 5 of the Social Security (Additional General Provisions Child Support Payments) Amendment Regulations 2023 (SL 2023/188).

prov.label: 294A
prov.heading: Additional general provisions child support payments

para.text: This subpart specifies kinds of child support payments.
They are specified for the purposes of paragraph (j) of the definition of general provisions child support payment in Schedule 2 of the Act.
The effect of this subpart specifying them for those purposes is that they are additional general provisions child support payments.

history.history-note: Regulation 294A: inserted, on 22 August 2023, by regulation 5 of the Social Security (Additional General Provisions Child Support Payments) Amendment Regulations 2023 (SL 2023/188).

subpart.crosshead: Kinds of child support payment specified by subpart

history.history-note: Heading: inserted, on 22 August 2023, by regulation 5 of the Social Security (Additional General Provisions Child Support Payments) Amendment Regulations 2023 (SL 2023/188).

prov.label: 294B
prov.heading: Child support payment to relevant non-matched beneficiary

prov.body.subprov.crosshead: Kind of child support payment specified

para.text: This regulation specifies the kind of child support payment that is a child support payment to a relevant non-matched beneficiary.

prov.body.subprov.crosshead: Meaning of child support payment to a relevant non-matched beneficiary

para.text: In this regulation, a child support payment to a relevant non-matched beneficiary, for a person, means a payment that is—
money received by the person that is child support; and
disclosed to MSD under a specified approved information sharing agreement (as that term is defined in Schedule 2 of the Act); and
made to a person who is a relevant non-matched beneficiary when MSD finishes processing the disclosed information associated with the payment.

prov.body.subprov.crosshead: Meaning of relevant non-matched beneficiary

para.text: In this regulation, a relevant non-matched beneficiary means a person who—
is entitled to receive a benefit, or any other assistance under the Act, that is not matched assistance; and
is not receiving matched assistance.

prov.body.subprov.crosshead: Meanings of other defined terms

para.text: In this regulation,—
matched assistance means any benefit, or other assistance under the Act, that is—
jobseeker support; or
sole parent support; or
a supported living payment on the ground of restricted work capacity or total blindness, under section 34 of the Act; or
a supported living payment on the ground of caring for another person, under section 40 of the Act; or
a youth payment; or
a young parent payment; or
an emergency benefit; or
an accommodation supplement under subpart 10 of Part 2 of the Act; or
childcare assistance under subpart 12 of Part 2 of the Act; or
a disability allowance under subpart 14 of Part 2 of the Act; or
temporary additional support under subpart 16 of Part 2 of the Act; or

New Zealand superannuation (generally abolished rate for person with non-qualifying spouse or partner) under clause 7 of Schedule 1AA, and clause 1 of Part 2 of Schedule 1, of the New Zealand Superannuation and Retirement Income Act 2001; or
a veteran’s pension under both of the following provisions of the Veterans’ Support Act 2014:
section 164 (entitlement to veteran’s pension under War Pensions Act 1954 by reason of infirmity); and
clause 23 of Schedule 1 (generally abolished rate for veterans with non-qualifying spouse or partner); or
a community costs payment under the Community Costs Programme (saved by clause 21 of Schedule 1 of the Act as if it were approved and established under section 101 of the Act); or
employment transition assistance under the Employment Transition Programme (saved by clause 21 of Schedule 1 of the Act as if it were approved and established under section 101 of the Act); or
a special benefit under clause 19 of Schedule 1 of the Act (which saves a special benefit under the Social Security (Working for Families) Amendment Act 2004)

processing, for information disclosed to MSD under a specified approved information sharing agreement and associated with a child support payment, means MSD determining on the basis of the information, and whether or not an automated electronic system is used under subpart 5A of Part 6 of the Act, whether the payment is or affects any person’s assets, income, or other means of any kind (however described), for the purposes of, or for any purpose related to,—
a benefit; or
any other assistance under the Act.

history.history-note: Regulation 294B: inserted, on 22 August 2023, by regulation 5 of the Social Security (Additional General Provisions Child Support Payments) Amendment Regulations 2023 (SL 2023/188).

part.heading: Provision relating to these regulations as made

prov.heading: Childcare assistance: approvals of out-of-school programme or proposed out-of-school programme

para.text: This clause applies to an approval of an out-of-school programme or proposed out-of-school programme—

given by the chief executive before 26 November 2018 under regulation 25 of the Social Security (Childcare Assistance) Regulations 2004 (or required under regulation 30 of those regulations to be taken to have been so given); and
in force immediately before 26 November 2018.
The approval continues, and may be suspended or revoked under regulation 46, as if it had been given under regulation 45.

part.heading: Provision relating to Social Security (Childcare Assistance—Cohort Entry Policy) Amendment Regulations 2019

history.history-note: Schedule 1 Part 2: inserted, on 1 January 2020, by regulation 6 of the Social Security (Childcare Assistance—Cohort Entry Policy) Amendment Regulations 2019 (LI 2019/264).

part.crosshead: Eligibility for payment of OSCAR subsidy of child who is at least 4 years old, and enrolled in and attending school that has cohort entry policy in place

prov.heading: Effect of revocation of regulation 38(c)﻿(ii)

para.text: The revocation of regulation 38(c)﻿(ii) on 1 January 2020 does not affect a child’s eligibility for payment of an OSCAR subsidy under that regulation as in force before that date.

prov.label: 2A
prov.heading: Effect of revocation of regulation 179A

para.text: The revocation of regulation 179A on 23 November 2020 does not affect the operation of that regulation as in force before that date.

history.history-note: Schedule 1 clause 2A: inserted, on 13 March 2020, by regulation 6 of the Social Security (Exemption from Stand Down—Coronavirus COVID-19) Amendment Regulations 2020 (LI 2020/34).

part.heading: Provision relating to Social Security (Emergency Housing and Transitional Housing) Amendment Regulations 2020

history.history-note: Schedule 1 Part 3: inserted, on 30 March 2020, by regulation 9 of the Social Security (Emergency Housing and Transitional Housing) Amendment Regulations 2020 (LI 2020/20).

prov.heading: Application of regulation 71(5)﻿(d)

para.text: Regulation 71(5)﻿(d) applies to a transitional housing contribution (as that term is defined in regulation 3(1))—
whether it arose before, on, or after 30 March 2020; but
only to the extent that it is, on or after 30 March 2020, payable as an expense by, or recoverable as a debt from, a person provided that housing because of the assistance concerned.

history.history-note: Schedule 1 clause 3: inserted, on 30 March 2020, by regulation 9 of the Social Security (Emergency Housing and Transitional Housing) Amendment Regulations 2020 (LI 2020/20).

part.heading: Provisions relating to Social Security (COVID-19—Temporary Additional Support and Expiry and Regrant of Benefits) Amendment Regulations 2020

history.history-note: Schedule 1 Part 4: inserted, on 30 March 2020, by regulation 6 of the Social Security (COVID-19—Temporary Additional Support and Expiry and Regrant of Benefits) Amendment Regulations 2020 (LI 2020/53).

prov.heading: Meaning of Amendment Regulations

para.text: In this Part, Amendment Regulations means the Social Security (COVID-19—Temporary Additional Support and Expiry and Regrant of Benefits) Amendment Regulations 2020.

history.history-note: Schedule 1 clause 4: inserted, on 30 March 2020, by regulation 6 of the Social Security (COVID-19—Temporary Additional Support and Expiry and Regrant of Benefits) Amendment Regulations 2020 (LI 2020/53).

prov.heading: Application of regulation 67(1)﻿(aa), (1A), (1B), and (1C)

para.text: Regulation 67(1)﻿(aa), (1A), (1B), and (1C) applies only to temporary additional support granted after the commencement of the Amendment Regulations.

history.history-note: Schedule 1 clause 5: inserted, on 30 March 2020, by regulation 6 of the Social Security (COVID-19—Temporary Additional Support and Expiry and Regrant of Benefits) Amendment Regulations 2020 (LI 2020/53).

prov.heading: Application of regulation 189(d) and (e)

para.text: Regulation 189(d) and (e) prescribes circumstances in which MSD may, after the commencement of the Amendment Regulations, and under section 334 of the Act, consider an exemption from expiry under section 332 of the Act for a specified benefit that is—
a specified benefit granted before that commencement, but the expiry date for which is on or after that commencement; or
a specified benefit granted on or after that commencement.

history.history-note: Schedule 1 clause 6: inserted, on 30 March 2020, by regulation 6 of the Social Security (COVID-19—Temporary Additional Support and Expiry and Regrant of Benefits) Amendment Regulations 2020 (LI 2020/53).

part.heading: Provisions relating to Social Security (COVID-19 Recovery—Exemption from Stand Down, and Expiry and Regrant of Benefits) Amendment Regulations 2020

history.history-note: Schedule 1 Part 5: inserted, on 10 August 2020, by regulation 8 of the Social Security (COVID-19 Recovery—Exemption from Stand Down, and Expiry and Regrant of Benefits) Amendment Regulations 2020 (LI 2020/179).

prov.heading: Effect of revocation of regulation 179B

para.text: The revocation of regulation 179B on 25 July 2021 does not affect the operation of that regulation as in force before that date.

history.history-note: Schedule 1 clause 7: inserted, on 10 August 2020, by regulation 8 of the Social Security (COVID-19 Recovery—Exemption from Stand Down, and Expiry and Regrant of Benefits) Amendment Regulations 2020 (LI 2020/179).

prov.heading: Application of regulation 189(f)

para.text: In this clause, commencement means the start of the day that is the earlier of—
the day on which the Epidemic Preparedness (COVID-19) Notice 2020 expires or is revoked; and
24 September 2020.

Regulation 189(f) prescribes circumstances in which MSD may, after the commencement, and under section 334 of the Act, consider an exemption from expiry under section 332 of the Act for a specified benefit that is—
a specified benefit granted before the commencement, but the expiry date for which is on or after the commencement; or
a specified benefit granted on or after the commencement.

history.history-note: Schedule 1 clause 8: inserted, on 10 August 2020, by regulation 8 of the Social Security (COVID-19 Recovery—Exemption from Stand Down, and Expiry and Regrant of Benefits) Amendment Regulations 2020 (LI 2020/179).

prov.heading: Effect of revocation of regulation 189(f)

para.text: The revocation of regulation 189(f) on 30 March 2021 does not affect the operation of that regulation as in force before that date.

history.history-note: Schedule 1 clause 9: inserted, on 10 August 2020, by regulation 8 of the Social Security (COVID-19 Recovery—Exemption from Stand Down, and Expiry and Regrant of Benefits) Amendment Regulations 2020 (LI 2020/179).

part.heading: Provision relating to Social Security (COVID-19—Childcare Assistance) Amendment Regulations 2020

history.history-note: Schedule 1 Part 6: inserted, on 25 August 2020, by regulation 5 of the Social Security (COVID-19—Childcare Assistance) Amendment Regulations 2020 (LI 2020/223).

prov.heading: Application of regulation 28(1)﻿(a), (3)﻿(aa), and (4)

para.text: Regulation 28(1)﻿(a), (3)﻿(aa), and (4) applies, on and after the commencement, on 25 August 2020, of the Social Security (COVID-19—Childcare Assistance) Amendment Regulations 2020, to a child who is eligible for childcare assistance regardless of whether the child became eligible for the assistance—
before that commencement; or
on or after that commencement.

history.history-note: Schedule 1 clause 10: inserted, on 25 August 2020, by regulation 5 of the Social Security (COVID-19—Childcare Assistance) Amendment Regulations 2020 (LI 2020/223).

history.history-note: Schedule 1 Part 7: inserted, on 1 July 2021, by section 16 of the Social Security (Financial Assistance for Caregivers) Amendment Act 2021 (2021 No 25).

para.text: The amendments to subpart 6 of Part 2 and Schedule 2 that are made by the Social Security (Financial Assistance for Caregivers) Amendment Act 2021 apply to a person—
who makes an application for childcare assistance for a child on or after 1 July 2021; or
whose application for childcare assistance for a child was made, but not decided under section 301 of the Act, before 1 July 2021.

history.history-note: Schedule 1 clause 11: inserted, on 1 July 2021, by section 16 of the Social Security (Financial Assistance for Caregivers) Amendment Act 2021 (2021 No 25).

para.text: The amendments to subpart 10 of Part 2 that are made by the Social Security (Financial Assistance for Caregivers) Amendment Act 2021 apply to a person—
who makes an application for temporary additional support for a child on or after 1 July 2021; or
whose application for temporary additional support for a child was made, but not decided under section 301 of the Act, before 1 July 2021.

prov.heading: Definitions for this Part

para.text: In this Part,—
amendments means the amendments made—
to these regulations; and
by Part 2 of the Amendment Act
Amendment Act means the Social Security (Accommodation Supplement) Amendment Act 2022
commencement means the commencement of the Amendment Act.

history.history-note: Schedule 1 clause 13: inserted, on 26 November 2022, by section 16(a) of the Social Security (Accommodation Supplement) Amendment Act 2022 (2022 No 65).

prov.heading: Amendments apply only to specified existing or new recipients or applicants

para.text: The amendments apply, after the commencement, only to—
a person who, immediately before the commencement, was entitled to receive, had applied for, and was receiving an accommodation supplement:
an applicant for an accommodation supplement whose application was made, but was not withdrawn or determined, before the commencement:
an applicant for an accommodation supplement whose application is made after the commencement.

history.history-note: Schedule 1 clause 14: inserted, on 26 November 2022, by section 16(a) of the Social Security (Accommodation Supplement) Amendment Act 2022 (2022 No 65).

part.heading: Provisions relating to Child Support (Pass On) Acts Amendment Act 2023 and Social Security (Additional General Provisions Child Support Payments) Amendment Regulations 2023

history.history-note: Schedule 1 Part 9: inserted, on 1 July 2023, by section 57(a) of the Child Support (Pass On) Acts Amendment Act 2023 (2023 No 27).
Schedule 1 Part 9 heading: amended, on 22 August 2023, by regulation 6(1) of the Social Security (Additional General Provisions Child Support Payments) Amendment Regulations 2023 (SL 2023/188).

prov.heading: Application of amendments

para.text: In this clause,—

amendments means the amendments made to these regulations by subpart 2 of Part 2 of the Child Support (Pass On) Acts Amendment Act 2023, and the amendments made to these regulations by the Social Security (Additional General Provisions Child Support Payments) Amendment Regulations 2023
formula assessment has the meaning in section 2(1) of the Child Support Act 1991.
The amendments apply only to child support payments based on child support in respect of a period (whether all, or any part, of a child support year) that starts on or after 1 July 2023.

The child support referred to in subclause (2) includes, but is not limited to, child support under a formula assessment or a voluntary agreement or an order of a court made or entered into before 1 July 2023.

Child support in respect of a period (whether all, or any part, of a child support year) that starts before 1 July 2023 is subject to these regulations as in force at the relevant time before 1 July 2023.

history.history-note: Schedule 1 clause 15: inserted, on 1 July 2023, by section 57(a) of the Child Support (Pass On) Acts Amendment Act 2023 (2023 No 27).

Schedule 1 clause 15(1) amendments: amended, on 22 August 2023, by regulation 6(2) of the Social Security (Additional General Provisions Child Support Payments) Amendment Regulations 2023 (SL 2023/188).

prov.heading: Application of amendments

para.text: In this clause,—
amendment regulations means the Social Security (Temporary Additional Support—North Island Weather Events TAA Programme) Amendment Regulations 2023
amendments means the amendments made to these regulations by the amendment regulations.
The amendments apply only to—

temporary additional support granted before, and payable on or after, the commencement of the amendment regulations (for example, after a recalculation under regulation 65 of the amount of support); and
temporary additional support granted after the commencement of the amendment regulations.

para.text: In this clause,—
amendment regulations means the Social Security (Temporary Additional Support—Severe Weather Events TAA Programme) Amendment Regulations 2023
amendments means the amendments made to these regulations by the amendment regulations.
The amendments apply only to—

temporary additional support granted before, and payable on or after, the commencement of the amendment regulations (for example, after a recalculation under regulation 65 of the amount of support); and
temporary additional support granted after the commencement of the amendment regulations.

history.history-note: Schedule 1 Part 12: inserted, on 1 April 2024, by regulation 6(a) of the Social Security (Temporary Additional Support—Severe Weather Events TAA Programme) Amendment Regulations 2024 (SL 2024/26).

prov.heading: Application of amendments

para.text: In this clause,—
amendment regulations means the Social Security (Temporary Additional Support—Severe Weather Events TAA Programme) Amendment Regulations 2024
amendments means the amendments made to these regulations by the amendment regulations.
The amendments apply only to—

temporary additional support granted before, and payable on or after, the commencement of the amendment regulations (for example, after a recalculation under regulation 65 of the amount of support); and
temporary additional support granted after the commencement of the amendment regulations.

history.history-note: Schedule 1 clause 18: inserted, on 1 April 2024, by regulation 6(a) of the Social Security (Temporary Additional Support—Severe Weather Events TAA Programme) Amendment Regulations 2024 (SL 2024/26).

part.heading: Provisions relating to Social Security (Emergency Housing) Amendment Regulations 2024

history.history-note: Schedule 1 Part 13: inserted, on 26 August 2024, by regulation 6(a) of the Social Security (Emergency Housing) Amendment Regulations 2024 (SL 2024/137).

prov.heading: Definitions for this Part

para.text: In this Part,—
amendment regulations means the Social Security (Emergency Housing) Amendment Regulations 2024
amendments means the amendments made to these regulations by the amendment regulations
emergency housing assistance means assistance of that kind paid to or for the credit of a person under—
the Special Needs Grants Programme (as defined in regulation 3(1), as in force immediately before 26 August 2024); or
the Emergency Housing Grants Programme (as defined in regulation 3(1), as in force on and after 26 August 2024)
emergency housing contribution has the meaning given to it in regulation 3(1) (as in force at the relevant time).

history.history-note: Schedule 1 clause 19: inserted, on 26 August 2024, by regulation 6(a) of the Social Security (Emergency Housing) Amendment Regulations 2024 (SL 2024/137).

prov.heading: Amendments apply only to assistance applied for on or after 26 August 2024

para.text: The amendments apply only to the following:
emergency housing assistance applied for on or after 26 August 2024:
an emergency housing contribution in respect of assistance of that kind.

history.history-note: Schedule 1 clause 20: inserted, on 26 August 2024, by regulation 6(a) of the Social Security (Emergency Housing) Amendment Regulations 2024 (SL 2024/137).

prov.heading: Saving for assistance applied for before 26 August 2024

para.text: The following continue to be subject to these regulations (as in force immediately before 26 August 2024):
emergency housing assistance—
applied for before 26 August 2024; and
granted before, on, or after that date:
an emergency housing contribution in respect of assistance of that kind.

history.history-note: Schedule 1 clause 21: inserted, on 26 August 2024, by regulation 6(a) of the Social Security (Emergency Housing) Amendment Regulations 2024 (SL 2024/137).

part.heading: Provisions relating to Social Security Amendment Act 2025

history.history-note: Schedule 1 Part 14: inserted, on 26 May 2025, by section 76(a) of the Social Security Amendment Act 2025 (2025 No 25).

prov.heading: Definitions for this Part

para.text: In this Part, unless the context otherwise requires,—
amendment Act means the Social Security Amendment Act 2025.

history.history-note: Schedule 1 clause 22: inserted, on 26 May 2025, by section 76(a) of the Social Security Amendment Act 2025 (2025 No 25).

prov.heading: Amendments related to expiry date for jobseeker support

prov.body.subprov.crosshead: Benefits to which amendments apply

para.text: The amendments made to regulation 187 by the amendment Act (the amendments) apply, on and after 1 July 2025, only to the following jobseeker support, and apply subject to subclauses (2) to (6):
jobseeker support that commences or, as the case may be, last commences, on or after 1 July 2025; and
jobseeker support that commences or, as the case may be, last commences,—
on or after 4 February 2025; but
before 1 July 2025; and
jobseeker support that commences or, as the case may be, last commences, before 4 February 2025; and
jobseeker support specified in clause 25(1)﻿(b).

Jobseeker support specified in subclause (1)﻿(a), (b), or (c) excludes jobseeker support if, immediately before 1 July 2025, the weekly income (other than any information share child support payment) of the person receiving that jobseeker support is calculated over 52 weeks under—
clauses 11 and 13 of Part 3 of Schedule 3 of the Act; and
regulation 205(1) of these regulations; and
the appropriate number of weeks specified for that benefit in item 1 of Schedule 6 of these regulations.

prov.body.subprov.crosshead: Example of jobseeker support specified in subclause (1)﻿(a), (b), (c), or (d)

para.text: Jobseeker support specified in subclause (1)﻿(a), (b), (c), or (d) includes, without limitation, jobseeker support that under section 33—
commences on the date that the youngest dependent child in a person’s (P’s) care turns 14 years old; and
replaces P’s sole parent support that expires on that date; and
is taken to be granted to P as if P had applied for it.

prov.body.subprov.crosshead: How amendments apply to jobseeker support specified in subclause (1)﻿(a)

para.text: The amendments apply to jobseeker support specified in subclause (1)﻿(a) only from the time that benefit commences or, as the case may be, last commences.

prov.body.subprov.crosshead: How amendments apply to jobseeker support specified in subclause (1)﻿(b)

para.text: The amendments apply to jobseeker support specified in subclause (1)﻿(b) as if its first expiry date under regulation 187 on and after 1 July 2025 is the date that is 26 weeks after that jobseeker support commences or, as the case may be, last commences.

prov.body.subprov.crosshead: How amendments apply to jobseeker support specified in subclause (1)﻿(c)

para.text: The amendments apply to jobseeker support specified in subclause (1)﻿(c) as follows:
it remains subject to regulation 187 as in force immediately before 4 February 2025; but
on its expiry date under that regulation 187, it ceases to be subject to that regulation 187, and instead becomes subject to regulation 187 as in force on and after 1 July 2025.

prov.body.subprov.crosshead: How amendments apply to jobseeker support specified in subclause (1)﻿(d)

para.text: The amendments apply to jobseeker support specified in subclause (1)﻿(d) as follows:
it remains subject to regulation 187 as in force immediately before 1 July 2025 until the end of the 52 weeks specified in clause 25(1)﻿(b); but
at the end of those 52 weeks, it ceases to be subject to that regulation 187, and instead becomes subject to regulation 187 as in force on and after 1 July 2025.

history.history-note: Schedule 1 clause 23: inserted, on 26 May 2025, by section 76(a) of the Social Security Amendment Act 2025 (2025 No 25).

prov.heading: Saving of old regulation 190 for reapplications in progress

para.text: This clause applies to a beneficiary—
granted jobseeker support before 1 July 2025; and
notified or advised under section 335 of the Act that their entitlement to that benefit will cease unless the beneficiary reapplies for that benefit and it is regranted; and
whose reapplication for regrant of jobseeker support was, on 1 July 2025, neither withdrawn nor finally determined.
The reapplication remains subject to regulation 190 as in force immediately before 1 July 2025.
Any jobseeker support regranted—
commences or, as the case may be, last commences, on or after 1 July 2025; and therefore
is under clause 23(1)﻿(a) subject to the amendments made to regulation 187.

history.history-note: Schedule 1 clause 24: inserted, on 26 May 2025, by section 76(a) of the Social Security Amendment Act 2025 (2025 No 25).

prov.heading: Amendment relating to how person’s weekly income (other than any information share child support payment) is calculated

prov.body.subprov.crosshead: Jobseeker support to which amendment applies

para.text: The amendment made to Schedule 6 by the amendment Act (the amendment) applies only to the following jobseeker support, and applies subject to subclauses (2) to (4):
jobseeker support that commences or, as the case may be, last commences, on or after 1 July 2025:

jobseeker support that commences or, as the case may be, last commences, before 1 July 2025, if, and only if, immediately before 1 July 2025, the weekly income (other than any information share child support payment) of the person receiving that jobseeker support is calculated over 52 weeks under—
clauses 11 and 13 of Part 3 of Schedule 3 of the Act; and
regulation 205(1) of these regulations; and
the appropriate number of weeks specified for that benefit in item 1 of Schedule 6 of these regulations.

prov.body.subprov.crosshead: Example of jobseeker support specified in subclause (1)﻿(a) or (b)

para.text: Jobseeker support specified in subclause (1)﻿(a) or (b) includes, without limitation, jobseeker support that under section 33—
commences on the date that the youngest dependent child in a person’s (P’s) care turns 14 years old; and
replaces P’s sole parent support that expires on that date; and
is taken to be granted to P as if P had applied for it.

prov.body.subprov.crosshead: When amendment applies to jobseeker support specified in subclause (1)﻿(a)

para.text: The amendment applies to jobseeker support specified in subclause (1)﻿(a) only from the time that benefit commences or last commences.

prov.body.subprov.crosshead: When amendment applies to jobseeker support specified in subclause (1)﻿(b)

para.text: The amendment applies to jobseeker support specified in subclause (1)﻿(b) only from the end of the 52 weeks specified in subclause (1)﻿(b).

history.history-note: Schedule 1 clause 25: inserted, on 26 May 2025, by section 76(a) of the Social Security Amendment Act 2025 (2025 No 25).

part.heading: Provisions relating to Social Security Amendment Regulations (No 3) 2025

history.history-note: Schedule 1 Part 15: inserted, on 2 March 2026, by regulation 8(a) of the Social Security Amendment Regulations (No 3) 2025 (SL 2025/306).

prov.heading: Interpretation

para.text: In this Part, amendment regulations means the Social Security Amendment Regulations (No 3) 2025.

history.history-note: Schedule 1 clause 26: inserted, on 2 March 2026, by regulation 8(a) of the Social Security Amendment Regulations (No 3) 2025 (SL 2025/306).

prov.heading: How MSD must decide temporary additional support application made, but not decided, before 2 March 2026

para.text: This clause applies if, before 2 March 2026,—
an application for temporary additional support was made; and
the application—
was not withdrawn; and
did not lapse; and
MSD did not make a decision under section 301 of the Act in relation to the application.

The amendments made to regulations 57, 66, and 71 by the amendment regulations do not apply for the purposes of the decision required to be made under section 301 of the Act in relation to the application.

history.history-note: Schedule 1 clause 27: inserted, on 2 March 2026, by regulation 8(a) of the Social Security Amendment Regulations (No 3) 2025 (SL 2025/306).

prov.heading: Transitional provision for persons granted temporary additional support before 2 March 2026

para.text: This clause applies to a person who—
was receiving temporary additional support immediately before 2 March 2026; or
was granted temporary additional support before 2 March 2026 that—
commences on or after 2 March 2026; or
immediately before 2 March 2026, was subject to a suspension.
The amendments made to regulations 57, 66, and 71 by the amendment regulations apply to the person for weeks starting on or after 2 March 2026.
This clause is subject to clause 27.

history.history-note: Schedule 1 clause 28: inserted, on 2 March 2026, by regulation 8(a) of the Social Security Amendment Regulations (No 3) 2025 (SL 2025/306).

prov.heading: How MSD must decide youth support payment application made, but not decided, before 2 March 2026

para.text: This clause applies if, before 2 March 2026,—
an application for a youth support payment was made; and
the application—
was not withdrawn; and
did not lapse; and
MSD did not make a decision under section 301 of the Act in relation to the application.
The amendments made to regulation 173 by the amendment regulations do not apply for the purposes of the decision required to be made under section 301 of the Act in relation to the application.

history.history-note: Schedule 1 clause 29: inserted, on 2 March 2026, by regulation 8(a) of the Social Security Amendment Regulations (No 3) 2025 (SL 2025/306).
